perturbation.original_text: 1.1 The Intellectual Property 1.1.1 Upon the terms and conditions hereinafter set forth, Party A hereby agrees to grant and Party B hereby agree to accept the right to use the intellectual property in PRC. The license under this Agreement is a non-exclusive, non-assignable and non-transferable license. 1.1.2 Party A owns the sole and exclusive right of the intellectual property, including any improvement, upgrades and derived products, no matter whether such products are created by Party A or Party B. The right and obligation under 1.1.2 of this article shall survive upon termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.1 The Intellectual Property 1.1.1 Upon the terms and conditions hereinafter set forth, Party A hereby agrees to grant and Party B hereby agree to accept the right to use the intellectual property in PRC. The license under this Agreement is a non-exclusive, non-assignable and non-transferable license.
perturbation.explanation: By removing clause 1.1.2, the agreement loses clarity regarding the ownership of improvements and derived products of the intellectual property. This omission contradicts the underlying assumption in other sections, specifically regarding Party A's rights and Party B's obligations upon termination, where the ownership of these derivatives is implied to remain with Party A. Now it is not clear whether Party A owns the improvement, upgrades, and derived products, since that has been removed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.2 Effect of Termination or Expiration Upon and after the expiration or termination of this Agreement, Party B shall not have all rights granted pursuant to this Agreement and will refrain from further direct or indirect use of the Intellectual Property.
perturbation.justification: The removal of clause 1.1.2, which explicitly stated that Party A owns improvements, upgrades, and derived products of the intellectual property, creates a contradiction with section 7.2. Section 7.2 states that upon termination, Party B shall not have all rights granted pursuant to this agreement and will refrain from further direct or indirect use of the Intellectual Property. Because it is no longer clearly stated that Party A owns improvements, upgrades, and derived products, it is no longer clear if Party B must refrain from using them upon termination. This creates an inconsistency and potential dispute.

perturbation.original_text: 2. Terms of Payment: Party B agrees to pay Party A the Intellectual Property License fees (the "License Fees") and the specified amount of the license fees and the form of payment are set forth in Appendix 2. Party A has the right to exempt the obligation of payment or adjust the amount of the License Fees set forth in Appendix 2 according to the actual circumstances from time to time.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Terms of Payment: Party B agrees to pay Party A the Intellectual Property License fees (the "License Fees"). Party A has the right to exempt the obligation of payment or adjust the amount of the License Fees according to the actual circumstances from time to time.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the reference to Appendix 2 where the specific amount of license fees and the form of payment are mentioned, this section becomes internally contradictory. While it states that Party B agrees to pay license fees, the details regarding the amount and payment method are omitted, making the obligation vague and unenforceable. This now creates a contradiction with Appendix 2, since we say in this location, we are reffering to the appendix, while it isn't.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Appendix 2 Account and payment method of License Fee Party B should pay for All Intellectual Property RMB 1,000,000 per year to Party A as license fee. Party B shall pay the license fee to the account designated by Party A before December 31 each year. Party A has the right to determine whether or not to exempt the Party B's obligation to pay license fee at its discretion.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the reference to Appendix 2, where the specific amount and payment method are detailed. This omission creates a contradiction with Appendix 2, which specifies the payment amount (RMB 1,000,000 per year) and the payment deadline (December 31 each year). The altered Section 2 becomes vague and unenforceable regarding the license fees, directly conflicting with the specific terms outlined in Appendix 2.

perturbation.original_text: 15. Appendices: The Appendices referred to in this Agreement are an integral part of this Agreement and have the same legal effect as this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 15. Appendices: The Appendices referred to in this Agreement may provide additional information but do not form an integral part of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By stating the appendices don't form an integral part of the agreement, but the other sections refer to the appendices as having an effect on key aspects of the contract like payment terms. This creates a direct in-text contradiction and makes the overall contract ambiguous and less enforceable, since we are stating that they may provide additional information, but it doesn't fully affect the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Terms of Payment: Party B agrees to pay Party A the Intellectual Property License fees (the "License Fees"). Party A has the right to exempt the obligation of payment or adjust the amount of the License Fees according to the actual circumstances from time to time.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the nature of the appendices, stating they 'do not form an integral part of this Agreement,' while the contradicted text (Section 2) indicates that Party A can adjust payment obligations based on the 'actual circumstances,' which could be detailed within the appendices. If the appendices aren't an integral part of the agreement, then Section 2's reference to their content impacting payment becomes contradictory, creating ambiguity about whether the appendices' stipulations are enforceable or relevant to payment terms.
